title: Add a shape-constrained expert and stack with an AUC-focused metalearner
summary: Current gains come from flexible trees plus heuristic rank blending.
rationale: Current gains come from flexible trees plus heuristic rank blending. A monotone/interaction-constrained expert can encode physical priors like older tires and worse latent wear not reducing pit risk all else equal, while an AUC-focused Super Learner can combine that expert with existing models more cleanly than fixed grids.
implementation_hint: Train either monotone-constrained XGBoost or an EBM on physically ordered features (`TyreLife`, latent wear, degradation rate, laps left, race progress) with restricted interactions; generate OOF predictions and feed all base OOFs into a convex metalearner chosen to maximize ROC AUC rather than logloss.
expected_effect: Often adds a small but useful low-correlation signal and makes final ensembling less ad hoc than raw/rank weight sweeps.
risk: Monotone priors can be wrong in wet/SC-like edge cases, and a metalearner can leak if base OOF predictions are not generated on identical folds.
